title: Seeds, Replay, Settings, Save Data, and Migration · Program Brief: Action Replay
summary: A seed is saved without algorithm version, action times drive logic, JSON fields are trusted, and corrupted data erases the previous session. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the action replay increment in versioned replay and safe-state schema. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Replay logical commands and algorithm steps independently from wall-clock animation timing.
Record generator and solver versions, seed, dimensions, policies, assistance, action sequence, and deterministic state identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 2843 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate replay identity, logical actions, bounded JSON state, atomic replacement, and version migration; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Action Replay, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to versioned replay and safe-state schema.

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-action-replay — Replay logical commands and algorithm steps independently from wall-clock animation timing.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-determinism-replay-save-4
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-determinism-replay-save-4.
# Case 1655: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def worked_maze_determinism_replay_save_4(recorded_actions: int = 36, replayed_actions: int = 36, schema_version: int = 3, invalid_fields: int = 0, replay_digest_match: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "gridworked4",
        "topology1655",
        "search4dekrow",
        "eventworked41655",
        "access1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if recorded_actions < 0 or replayed_actions < 0:
        return False, replayed_actions, "replay-count"
    if replayed_actions != recorded_actions:
        return False, replayed_actions, "action-reconciliation"
    if schema_version < 1 or invalid_fields > 0:
        return False, invalid_fields, "schema-validation"
    if not replay_digest_match:
        return False, replayed_actions, "replay-divergence"
    assert replayed_actions == recorded_actions
    return True, replayed_actions, "versioned-replay-state"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-action-replay-x7iqdb
# Competency: maze-action-replay.
# Case 5692: a museum kiosk accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path.
def evidence_maze_action_replay_x7iqdb(recorded_actions: int = 36, replayed_actions: int = 36, schema_version: int = 3, invalid_fields: int = 0, replay_digest_match: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_x7iqdb = "x7iqdb-bdqi7x-5692"
    assert evidence_variant_x7iqdb
    evidence_axes_x7iqdb = (
        "gridx7iqdb",
        "topology5692",
        "searchbdqi7x",
        "eventx7iqdb5692",
        "access5692x7iqdb",
        "recoverybdqi7x5692",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_x7iqdb)) == 6
    if recorded_actions < 0 or replayed_actions < 0:
        return False, replayed_actions, "replay-count"
    if replayed_actions != recorded_actions:
        return False, replayed_actions, "action-reconciliation"
    if schema_version < 1 or invalid_fields > 0:
        return False, invalid_fields, "schema-validation"
    if not replay_digest_match:
        return False, replayed_actions, "replay-divergence"
    assert replayed_actions == recorded_actions
    return True, replayed_actions, "versioned-replay-state"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Replay logical commands and algorithm steps independently from wall-clock animation timing.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Action Replay model · Theory Action Replay, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Action Replay precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Action Replay model · Theory Action Replay, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 3557 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate replay identity, logical actions, bounded JSON state, atomic replacement, and version migration; the prior good state must survive failure; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Replay logical commands and algorithm steps independently from wall-clock animation timing.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Action Replay model · Theory Action Replay, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Recorded callback timestamps are the authoritative game logic.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-action-replay-x7iqdb.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-action-replay-x7iqdb(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_action_replay_x7iqdb\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?recorded_actions < 0 or replayed_actions < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?replayed_actions != recorded_actions)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?schema_version < 1 or invalid_fields > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not replay_digest_match)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using replay and logical plus specific case evidence.
